que: What is the primary purpose of the Expanded widget in Flutter?
options: To expand a child to fill available space in a Row, Column, or Flex
To add padding around a child
To align a child to the bottom

que: Expanded is commonly used to create what kind of layouts?
In which parent widgets can Expanded be used?
What is the default value of flex in Expanded?
Which parameter of Expanded is required?
What does the flex parameter in Expanded represent?
options: The flex factor determining how much space this child takes relative to others

que: What text is displayed in the first Expanded widget in the example Row?
What text is displayed in the second Expanded widget in the example Row?
If two Expanded widgets in a Row both have the default flex value, how is the available space shared?
options: First gets all, second gets none
Second gets all, first gets none
They do not take any space

que: what is a common use of Expanded?
options: To make widgets share available space equally
To stack widgets on top of each other

que: Which widget is suggested to combine with Expanded for more control over space distribution?
Which is listed as something to avoid with Expanded?
options: Using Expanded outside of Row, Column, or Flex

que: What is another thing to avoid with Expanded?
options: Wrapping only one child in Expanded
Wrapping all children in Expanded unless you want them to share space equally

que: In which direction does Expanded allocate space when used inside a Row?
In which direction does Expanded allocate space when used inside a Column?
What happens if one Expanded has flex 2 and the other has flex 1 in the same Row?
options: The second gets twice the space of the first
The first gets twice the space of the second

que: Which of the following is NOT mentioned as a valid parent for Expanded?
What is the main difference between Expanded and a normal child in a Row?
options: Expanded forces the child to take up remaining available space according to its flex
Expanded adds a border around the child

que: Which statement best summarizes the Expanded widget?
options: A widget that centers its child within its parent
A widget that expands a child of Row, Column, or Flex to fill available space based on a flex factor
A widget that scrolls its child
A widget that overlays children on top of each other
